name: agent-orchestrator

name: agentic-capability

description_normalized: Design advisor for choosing the right Claude Code extension mechanism before building anything.

name: ansible-fleet
description_raw: Manage and audit the Ansible-provisioned Mac fleet across David's machines (AppyDave group) and client machines (e.g. filtcore for Lars). Use when user says "audit fleet", "audit the machines", "check machine drift", "which machines have X", "add capability to ansible", "add this to the fleet", "remove from fleet", "deprecate capability", "ansible playbook", "fleet config", "group_vars", "host_vars", "fleet alignment", "machine out of sync", "what's on all my machines", "compare machines", "fleet inventory", "provision machine". The skill has three capabilities: (1) Fleet Audit — produce a drift/alignment report across all machines, (2) Capability Lifecycle — add/promote/deprecate a capability with Ansible-compatible classification, (3) Config Variation Management — explain where a setting lives (all/group/host).
description_normalized: Manage and audit the Ansible-provisioned Mac fleet across David's machines (AppyDave group) and client machines (e.g.

name: app-query
description_raw: Get files from a specific app using glob categories defined in context.globs.json. Use when user says: "get files from X app", "show me X's backend", "load X docs", "what apps have context", "list globs for X", "app files for X", "codebase of X", "understand X app", "X services", "X components", "X frontend", "X api", "X routes", "X tests", "X config", "all RVETS backend code". Requires context.globs.json in the project root (generated by system-context skill).

name: apps-registry
description_raw: Read, write, and shape the canonical AppyDave app registry at ~/.config/appydave/apps.json. Use when the user says: "add app", "register app", "retire app", "archive app", "list apps", "find app", "what apps do I have", "apps by family", "apps by runtime", "matrix data", "registry stats", "apps for [machine]", "update app", "move app to family", "validate registry", or "migrate registry".
description_normalized: Read, write, and shape the canonical AppyDave app registry at ~/.config/appydave/apps.json.

name: appydave-thumbnail
description_raw: Generate AppyDave YouTube thumbnail concepts from a transcript and one or more focal points, then generate finished thumbnails via Kie.ai with hook text composed into the image from the start. Typography-first brand — Bebas Neue hook text, ghost watermarks, warm cream + dark brown palette. Use when creating thumbnails for AppyDave YouTube videos, or when the user says 'thumbnail', 'generate thumbnails', 'thumbnail concepts', or wants to visualise focal points from a transcript as YouTube images.
description_normalized: Generate AppyDave YouTube thumbnail concepts from a transcript and one or more focal points, then generate finished thumbnails via Kie.ai with hook text composed into the image from the start.

name: architectural-review
description_raw: Structural and design quality review of system architecture. Different from code review — this asks "is this system well-structured for growth?" not "is this code correct?". Use when user says "architectural review", "system design review", "is this ready to scale", "structural review", "before adding a major feature", "God class", "too many responsibilities", "design review", "is this maintainable", "refactor planning", "before building on top", "architecture audit", "is this well designed", "responsibility review". Does NOT replace code-quality-audit or test-quality-audit — it adds a third lens on top of them.
description_normalized: Structural and design quality review of system architecture.

name: baku
description_raw: Sync and manage Baku app zips from claude.ai (claude-ship exports) to ~/Downloads/baku-2.0/. name: capture-context

name: code-quality-audit

name: codebase-audit

For a layer-aware full system health check use appydave:system-audit instead. name: conversation-triage

name: create-appystack
description_raw: Create a new AppyStack (RVETS) project from scratch using the create-appystack CLI. RVETS = React 19 + Vite 7 + Express 5 + TypeScript + Socket.io — npm workspaces monorepo with client/, server/, shared/ packages. Trigger on: "create a new AppyStack app", "scaffold a new RVETS project", "new AppyStack project", "create-appystack", "new full-stack app with AppyStack", "spin up an AppyStack". DO NOT invoke if the current working directory is already an AppyStack project — detected by presence of .claude/skills/recipe/ in CWD. Inside an existing AppyStack project, the "recipe" skill handles feature scaffolding instead.
description_normalized: Create a new AppyStack (RVETS) project from scratch using the create-appystack CLI.

name: dark-factory-catalog
description_raw: Catalog and assess skills, agents, workflows, commands, templates, and hooks across the 13 dark-factory cluster repos (BMAD-METHOD, gstack, Superpowers, Agent Skills Osmani, Compound Engineering, Compound Knowledge, Everything Claude Code, Ruflo, gbrain, Spec Kit, GSD, POEM, AppyDave Plugins). Distill ~1000+ raw artifacts down to ~50 unified skills under David's system. Use when user says "dark factory catalog", "catalog the repos", "recon BMAD" or "recon any specific repo", "discover artifacts", "discover skills across repos", "tag artifact stages", "evaluate compound engineering", "distill testing skills", "distill the catalog", "catalog self-improvement", "factory agent assessor", "assess skills across repos", "build the catalog", or any operation on the dark-factory cluster artifact corpus. Six capabilities (recon / discover / tag / evaluate / distill / self-improvement) — each invokable independently. Schema is NOT defined upfront — schema discovery is a first-class capability.
description_normalized: Catalog and assess skills, agents, workflows, commands, templates, and hooks across the 13 dark-factory cluster repos (BMAD-METHOD, gstack, Superpowers, Agent Skills Osmani, Compound Engineering, Compound Knowledge, Everything Claude Code, Ruflo, gbrain, Spec Kit, GSD, POEM, AppyDave Plugins).

name: delivery-review
description_raw: Orchestrate parallel delivery review across 6 quality dimensions. Launches review-blind-hunter, review-edge-case-hunter, review-acceptance-auditor, review-architecture, review-code-quality, and review-unit-tests as background agents, then triages and synthesizes findings into a unified verdict. Use when: 'delivery review', 'review this PR', 'review my changes', 'code review all dimensions', 'full review', 'review before merge', 'is this ready to ship'.
description_normalized: Orchestrate parallel delivery review across 6 quality dimensions.

name: doc-review
description_raw: Orchestrate parallel documentation review across 6 quality dimensions. Launches doc-review:coherence, doc-review:gaps, doc-review:completeness, doc-review:topology, doc-review:prose, and doc-review:crossref as background agents, then triages and synthesizes their findings. Use when user mentions: 'review docs', 'doc review', 'review this brain', 'documentation quality', 'full doc audit', 'review all dimensions', 'run doc review'.
description_normalized: Orchestrate parallel documentation review across 6 quality dimensions.

name: doc-review-coherence
description_raw: Review documentation for coherence — find contradictions between documents, tonal shifts suggesting different authors or time periods, claims that may have drifted from truth, and implicit assumptions that conflict. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (CH dimension). Use when user mentions: 'check coherence', 'find contradictions', 'doc contradictions', 'content drift', 'tonal consistency', 'do docs agree'.
description_normalized: Review documentation for coherence — find contradictions between documents, tonal shifts suggesting different authors or time periods, claims that may have drifted from truth, and implicit assumptions that conflict.

name: doc-review-completeness
description_raw: Review documentation for completeness — check whether docs deliver what they promise, map stated intent to actual content, and flag scope gaps and scope creep. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (CA dimension). Use when user mentions: 'check completeness', 'does it deliver', 'scope check', 'intent vs reality', 'does it cover everything', 'scope gaps', 'scope creep'.
description_normalized: Review documentation for completeness — check whether docs deliver what they promise, map stated intent to actual content, and flag scope gaps and scope creep.

name: doc-review-crossref
description_raw: Review documentation cross-reference integrity — verify provenance chains, check that examples match reality, validate index-to-file sync, and confirm version references are current. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (XR dimension). Use when user mentions: 'check cross-references', 'provenance chain', 'examples accurate', 'index sync', 'references current', 'version check', 'source attribution'.
description_normalized: Review documentation cross-reference integrity — verify provenance chains, check that examples match reality, validate index-to-file sync, and confirm version references are current.

name: doc-review-gaps
description_raw: Review documentation for gaps and loose ends — find missing sections, dead links, orphaned files, dangling references, undefined terms, and promised content that was never written. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (GL dimension). Use when user mentions: 'find gaps', 'missing docs', 'dead links', 'orphaned files', 'loose ends', 'what is missing', 'dangling references'.
description_normalized: Review documentation for gaps and loose ends — find missing sections, dead links, orphaned files, dangling references, undefined terms, and promised content that was never written.

name: doc-review-prose
description_raw: Review documentation prose and uniformity — check terminology consistency, formatting conventions, voice, DRY (no duplicated content), readability, and template adherence. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (PU dimension). Use when user mentions: 'check prose', 'terminology consistency', 'formatting check', 'doc style', 'duplicated content', 'DRY docs', 'voice consistency', 'template compliance'.
description_normalized: Review documentation prose and uniformity — check terminology consistency, formatting conventions, voice, DRY (no duplicated content), readability, and template adherence.

name: doc-review-topology
description_raw: Review documentation topology and structure — check file organization, grouping coherence, separation of concerns, composability, navigation paths, INDEX.md accuracy, and directory taxonomy. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (TS dimension). Use when user mentions: 'check structure', 'file organization', 'doc topology', 'index accuracy', 'navigation', 'directory structure', 'are docs well organized'.
description_normalized: Review documentation topology and structure — check file organization, grouping coherence, separation of concerns, composability, navigation paths, INDEX.md accuracy, and directory taxonomy.

description_normalized: Interview the user through a structured plan schema (Stack, In Scope, Out of Scope, Definition of Done, Acceptance Criteria, Key References) and produce two artifacts — a plan markdown document and a paste-ready 1-line /goal condition for Claude Code's autonomous goal feature (v2.1.139+, max 4000 chars).

name: mocha
description_raw: Mocha — the view renderer in the Mochaccino ecosystem. Takes JSON data files (from Peter) and renders self-contained HTML views using the AppyDave brand. name: omi-extract-haiku
description_raw: Extract and classify OMI conversation transcripts using Haiku model via the Claude Code harness (Max plan credits — no external API, no Gemini quota). Spawns parallel Haiku background agents for isolated per-batch classification. Adds extraction_model: haiku to distinguish from Gemini-extracted files. Use when: 'extract with haiku', 'haiku extract', 'omi haiku', 'intake extract haiku', 'compare extraction models', 'test haiku on omi files', 'run haiku extractor'.
description_normalized: Extract and classify OMI conversation transcripts using Haiku model via the Claude Code harness (Max plan credits — no external API, no Gemini quota).

name: omi-fetch
description_raw: Fetch conversations and transcripts from the OMI wearable device API. Use when user says "get omi transcripts", "what did I record", "omi conversations", "fetch from omi", "tonight's recordings", "what's in omi", "pull omi data", "show me what omi captured", "omi action items", "what did omi hear", "omi memories", or any request to retrieve voice recordings or transcripts from the OMI pendant device.
description_normalized: Fetch conversations and transcripts from the OMI wearable device API.

name: omi-query
description_raw: Query OMI wearable transcripts by brain, routing type, activity, or recency. Use when user says: "what did I record about X", "search omi for X", "omi backlog", "brain-update backlog", "what's pending brain updates", "what did omi capture about X", "recent omi sessions", "omi this week", "omi sessions mentioning X", "show me omi todo items", "what did I learn this week", "omi til entries", "omi planning sessions". Reads frontmatter tags written by omi-extract. Requires omi-extract to have run first.
description_normalized: Query OMI wearable transcripts by brain, routing type, activity, or recency.

name: omi-schedule
description_raw: Manage the omi-sync launchd schedule on macOS. Use when user says "update omi schedule", "change omi sync times", "when does omi sync run", "set omi to run every X hours", "show omi schedule", "omi cron", "omi launchd", "update omi plist", or any request to view or change when the omi-sync agent fires. Generates/updates ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.appydave.omi-sync.plist and regenerates _omi-sync-schedule.json (in $OMI_INTAKE_DIR) as a human-readable observation file.
description_normalized: Manage the omi-sync launchd schedule on macOS.

name: peter
description_raw: Peter — data specialist for the Mochaccino ecosystem. Manages the Providence data lifecycle: create, refresh, snapshot, fork, and discover JSON data shapes. Part of the Mochaccino trio (with Mochaccino and Shelly). Use when Mochaccino asks Peter to create or update data, or when user says "ask Peter to", "update the data", "refresh the JSON", "create a data shape", "snapshot this", "fork the data", "Peter can you", "what does the data look like". All data files live in .mochaccino/data/ and follow strict schema conventions.
description_normalized: Peter — data specialist for the Mochaccino ecosystem.

name: prompt-injection-scanner
description_raw: Scan untrusted content for prompt injection attacks before it enters any pipeline. Wraps content in an isolated Opus agent for model-pinned, auditable sanitization. Use when: 'scan this for injection', 'sanitize this content', 'check this before processing', 'is this safe to process?', 'scan incoming email / OMI / document / search result', or any time external content (email, web, relay, uploaded files, client documents) is about to be processed by a downstream skill or pipeline.
description_normalized: Scan untrusted content for prompt injection attacks before it enters any pipeline.

name: prose2models
description_raw: Converts evolving prose into structured machine-readable models (UX pages, user journeys, data models, presentation structures). Use when you have prose you need structured for downstream tools, or when user says "parse this", "extract structure", "model this", "turn this into a UX model", "extract user journey", "update the model", "version the model", "what shapes can I extract from this", or "what's the next step on the model". Supports extract, update, version, shape-discovery, and guidance commands.

name: ralphy
description_raw: Ralphy — David's Ralph Wiggum approach for autonomous batch campaigns on existing projects. Use when user says "ralphy", "run ralphy", "start a ralph campaign", "new campaign", "new PR", "new worktree", "start a wave", "plan this", "build this", "extend this", "add features", "run a coordinator loop", "what does ralphy do", "switch to analysis", "switch to development", "switch to content", "switch profile", or any mention of running an autonomous batch of work (planning, building, extending) on an existing project. Ralphy has four modes: Requirements, Plan, Build, Extend — and three campaign profiles: Development, Analysis, Content. He is directive — he reads current state and tells the user where things are and what's available.

name: review-blind-hunter

Use when: 'blind hunt', 'adversarial review', 'what am I missing', 'find hidden issues', 'cynical review'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (BH dimension).

Use when: 'code quality review', 'is this clean', 'quality check', 'naming review', 'DRY check'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (CQ dimension).
description_normalized: Assess naming, structure, DRY, separation of concerns, and code craftsmanship in changed code.

name: review-edge-case-hunter
description_raw: Exhaustive edge case analysis — walks every branching path and boundary condition, reports only unhandled cases. Method-driven path enumeration, not intuition. Use when: 'edge cases', 'boundary conditions', 'what could break', 'missing validations', 'path analysis'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (EC dimension).
description_normalized: Exhaustive edge case analysis — walks every branching path and boundary condition, reports only unhandled cases.

name: ruflo
description_raw: AppyDave's curated wrapper around Ruflo (claude-flow v3.5+). Codifies install, prune, memory-init, status, swarm-generation, AND dispatches the pre-flight swarm-check gate. When invoked with argument `swarm check` / `swarm-check` (e.g. via `/appydave:ruflo swarm check`) — OR when user types "should this be a swarm", "what shape for this work", "before we start", "pre-flight", "what topology" — IMMEDIATELY read /Users/davidcruwys/dev/ad/appydave-plugins/appydave/commands/ruflo-swarm-check.md and execute its 4-question gate to produce a 3-line decision (Decision / Write-zones / Ruflo wrap). Do not redirect the user — execute the gate inline. Also use this skill when the user says "install ruflo", "set up ruflo", "ruflo init", "prune ruflo", "ruflo prune", "ruflo customize", "what does ruflo install", "ruflo status", "ruflo agents", "orient", "what agents do I have", "how do I use these agents", "where do I start", "what should I do next", "generate swarm", "swarm this", "create swarm brief", "spawn swarm for", "ruflo generate", "build swarm for", "design a swarm to", or describes a task and asks how to run it as a Ruflo swarm.

Use when the user says: "add schedule", "register schedule", "list schedules", "what's running on cron", "what cron jobs do I have", "what's scheduled", "remove schedule", "schedule stats", "where does X run", "schedules on [machine]", "validate schedules", "audit schedules", or "show me my schedules".

name: screenshot-tour
description_raw: Automates full click-through screenshot documentation of web applications. Visits every route in a project's tour manifest, takes full-page screenshots with sequential naming, discovers new pages via nav/sidebar click-through, and presents a promotion gate to update the manifest with newly found routes. Also supports a standalone describe pass (Step 8) that reads each PNG and writes a visual description back into tour.yml — run any time after a tour to enrich the manifest with image content, turning it into a single readable document covering tour flow, metadata, and visual descriptions together. Supports a requirements mode (trigger: "requirements tour", "pre-rebuild tour", "capture requirements for...") that adds role tagging, per-screen requirements fields, user flow mapping, design system observations, and a REQUIREMENTS.md export — the handover artifact for UX designers and developers doing a rebuild. Use when the user wants to document an app visually, generate a screenshot walkthrough, capture all screens for mockups or docs, describe existing screenshots, or run a screenshot tour of AngelEye, FliHub, or any other web app.
description_normalized: Automates full click-through screenshot documentation of web applications.

name: session-checkpoint
description_raw: Checkpoint current task and workflow progress mid-session so it can be restored exactly. Use when user says "checkpoint", "save my progress", "checkpoint the workflow", "where am I up to", "save task state", "checkpoint before this next step", or when about to do a risky operation or context is filling up. Captures step-by-step progress through a plan or task list — NOT a session summary (use capture-context) and NOT durable knowledge (use knowledge-capture).
description_normalized: Checkpoint current task and workflow progress mid-session so it can be restored exactly.

name: shelly
description_raw: Shelly — Shape Librarian for the Mochaccino ecosystem. Maintains two catalogs: shape patterns (known data structures and when to use them) and UI components (reusable elements extracted from designs). Part of the Mochaccino trio (with Mochaccino and Peter). Use when Mochaccino asks Shelly to suggest a shape, or when user says "ask Shelly", "what shape fits", "Shelly can you", "add this to the library", "extract this component", "what components do we have", "find a pattern for", "which shape should I use for". Shelly grows over time — new patterns are added from real usage.
description_normalized: Shelly — Shape Librarian for the Mochaccino ecosystem.

name: skill-eval-loop
description_raw: Iteratively improve a Claude Code skill using automated eval loops. Run the skill against a test suite, score outputs with binary criteria, mutate SKILL.md, keep the winner, repeat until quality converges. Use when user says: "eval loop this skill", "refine this skill", "improve this skill", "run eval loop on [skill]", "optimize this skill", "run skill-eval-loop on", or wants to automatically improve a skill's reliability or output quality.
description_normalized: Iteratively improve a Claude Code skill using automated eval loops.

name: skill-miner
description_raw: Mines David's past Claude Code session transcripts (JSONL files in ~/.claude/projects/) for recurring patterns and promotes them into skill candidates that would improve his workflow. Use when David says "skill-miner", "mine my skills", "skill mining", "any skill candidates", "what should be a skill", "find skill patterns", "log this for skill-miner", "any new skills emerging", "skill miner scan". Two modes — scan (retrospective batch over recent JSONLs; default 7-day window) and log (hot-capture mid-session when David notices friction worth flagging). Patterns accumulate at ~/.claude/skill-miner/candidates.md, decay after 60+ days unseen, and promote to skill-stub drafts in promoted/ at 3+ instances within 90 days. Scoring is friction × frequency × cost, not raw repetition. Retrospective counterpart to AngelEye's live ingestion.
description_normalized: Mines David's past Claude Code session transcripts (JSONL files in ~/.claude/projects/) for recurring patterns and promotes them into skill candidates that would improve his workflow.

name: system-audit
description_raw: Full codebase health audit orchestrator — auto-detects the tech stack, maps the codebase into architectural layers, assigns the right specialist mix per layer, launches all layer reviews in parallel as background agents, then synthesises findings into a unified SYSTEM-HEALTH.md report. Unlike delivery-review (which is diff-scoped and story-focused), system-audit reads the full codebase and produces a severity-ranked health map. Use when user says: "system audit", "full audit", "codebase health check", "audit the whole system", "audit this codebase", "run a health check", "full system review", "review the whole codebase", "audit before onboarding", "what's wrong with this codebase". Supported stacks: Next.js App Router, RVETS (AppyStack), Baku (claude.ai/claude-ship), Astro, Rails, Ruby gem, AppySentinel. Accepts a manual layer manifest for any other stack.
description_normalized: Full codebase health audit orchestrator — auto-detects the tech stack, maps the codebase into architectural layers, assigns the right specialist mix per layer, launches all layer reviews in parallel as background agents, then synthesises findings into a unified SYSTEM-HEALTH.md report.

name: architect
description_raw: Software architect specializing in system design, technical strategy, and complex decision-making. Use when user mentions: "architecture", "system design", "tradeoffs", "design decision", "should I use X or Y", "how should I structure", "database schema", "API design", "scalability", "after 2+ failed attempts", "performance strategy"
description_normalized: Software architect specializing in system design, technical strategy, and complex decision-making.

name: code-reviewer
description_raw: Senior engineer conducting code review focusing on bugs, security, and maintainability. Use when user mentions: "review code", "code review", "review this PR", "find bugs", "find issues", "what's wrong with this code", "review my implementation", "before merge", "self-review", "review changes"
description_normalized: Senior engineer conducting code review focusing on bugs, security, and maintainability.

name: consultant-discovery
description_raw: Discover available external AI consultants and experts. Use when user asks: "who can help", "list consultants", "what experts", "show consultants", "available systems", "what consultants do you have", "what consultants are available", "which consultants", "available consultants", "which expert should I use", "help me choose an expert", "what external experts"
description_normalized: Discover available external AI consultants and experts.

name: delegation-router
description_raw: Route consultation requests to external AI experts via CLI. Use when user says: "consult [system] [expert]", "delegate to [expert]", "ask gpt", "ask codex", "second opinion from [system]", "get expert advice on", "consult architect", "consult security analyst", "consult code reviewer", "consult plan reviewer", "consult scope analyst", "ask the architect", "get the security analyst", "have the code reviewer", "check with"
description_normalized: Route consultation requests to external AI experts via CLI.

name: plan-reviewer
description_raw: Work plan review expert who catches gaps, ambiguities, and missing context before implementation. Use when user mentions: "review plan", "review this plan", "validate plan", "is this plan complete", "check my plan", "before starting", "plan validation", "review my implementation plan", "approve this plan"
description_normalized: Work plan review expert who catches gaps, ambiguities, and missing context before implementation.

name: scope-analyst
description_raw: Pre-planning consultant who analyzes requests before planning, catching ambiguities and hidden requirements. Use when user mentions: "scope", "analyze scope", "what am I missing", "clarify requirements", "before we start", "help me understand", "what should I consider", "scope analysis", "requirements unclear", "is this request clear"
description_normalized: Pre-planning consultant who analyzes requests before planning, catching ambiguities and hidden requirements.

name: security-analyst
description_raw: Security engineer specializing in application security, threat modeling, and vulnerability assessment. Use when user mentions: "security", "vulnerability", "is this secure", "threat model", "authentication review", "authorization", "SQL injection", "XSS", "OWASP", "sensitive data", "harden", "security audit", "exploit", "CVE"
description_normalized: Security engineer specializing in application security, threat modeling, and vulnerability assessment.

name: plugin-index
description_raw: Refresh INDEX.md — crawl all plugins, detect gaps, regenerate the master skill index
description_normalized: Refresh INDEX.md — crawl all plugins, detect gaps, regenerate the master skill index

name: ruflo-swarm-check
description_raw: Step-gate at task start — decide whether work is a sequential single coder, a fan-out swarm, or bespoke per-unit, and whether Ruflo MCP wrapping pays off. Run before pulling any new ticket or batch of tickets. Triggers when the user says "swarm check", "should this be a swarm", "what shape for this work", "before we start", "pre-flight", or is starting any non-trivial implementation batch.
description_normalized: Step-gate at task start — decide whether work is a sequential single coder, a fan-out swarm, or bespoke per-unit, and whether Ruflo MCP wrapping pays off.

name: curate-templates
description_raw: Pattern Extraction — review example slides and extract reusable patterns for the Solo Deck presentation template library.
description_normalized: Review example slides and extract reusable patterns for the presentation template library.

name: current-deck
description_raw: Mode Switcher — manages the current deck context for a conversation, determining where gather/solo-deck/scene-deck read from and write to.
description_normalized: Manage the current deck context (default, video, or project mode) for this conversation.

name: gather
description_raw: Data Collection — gather point-in-time facts from across the ecosystem (DAM, projects, storage, git) and save as structured collections.
description_normalized: Gather point-in-time facts from DAM and ecosystem systems and save as structured collections.

name: poem-slides
description_raw: Load POEM OS slide creation workflow and check progress
description_normalized: Load POEM OS slide creation workflow and check progress

description_normalized: Hot-reload plugin from source code (dev workflow)

description_normalized: Research command for Brand Dave content workflow.

description_normalized: Manage scene-level deck structure in Brand Dave workflow.

name: solo-deck

description_normalized: Manage solo presentation deck output in Brand Dave workflow.

description_normalized: Guide workflow for modifying/updating plugins in this repo

description_normalized: Creative thinking partner that refines rough ideas into concrete designs through dialogue.

description_normalized: Developer subagent for FliVideo implementation tasks.
